rationale: The participants are professional radiologists performing their core occupational duties: diagnosing pathologies from diagnostic images (chest X-rays), reviewing clinical history and lab results, and making treatment recommendations. The task description explicitly states 'Professional radiologists diagnose the presence of a given pathology in retrospective patient cases' — this is the substantive work of radiologists as an occupation, not a narrow isolated activity. While the study involves retrospective cases rather than real-time clinical work, the radiologists are performing the full scope of diagnostic radiology work across their workday.
